comment_text.sn9.11:1.1: This short discourse offers a concise version of meditation advice given in more detail at MN 19.
comment_text.sn9.11:1.2: Even ascetics in the forest can be consumed by bad thoughts.
comment_text.sn9.11:1.4: Trapped in a spiral of unwholesome thoughts, a meditator may feel guilty or worthless. But the deity has only compassion.
comment_text.sn9.11:2.1: This term has already appeared at SN 4.4:2.1, and plays a key role in dependent origination (SN 12.4:2.3). Their thoughts are irrational because they lead to the opposite of their goal, freedom.
comment_text.sn9.11:3.1: Unwholesome thoughts are replaced by wholesome ones (cf. MN 20:3.1). The meditator is not urged to stop thinking; quietness is a process.
comment_text.sn9.11:3.5: Suffering spurs on the practice, but the path itself is characterized by joy.

root_text.sn9.11:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ aññataro bhikkhu kosalesu viharati aññatarasmiṁ vanasaṇḍe.
root_text.sn9.11:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena so bhikkhu divāvihāragato pāpake akusale vitakke vitakketi, seyyathidaṁ—
root_text.sn9.11:1.4: Atha kho yā tasmiṁ vanasaṇḍe adhivatthā devatā tassa bhikkhuno anukampikā atthakāmā taṁ bhikkhuṁ saṁvejetukāmā yena so bhikkhu tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā taṁ bhikkhuṁ gāthāhi ajjhabhāsi:
root_text.sn9.11:4.1: Atha kho so bhikkhu tāya devatāya saṁvejito saṁvegamāpādīti.

translation_text.sn9.11:1.1: At one time one of the mendicants was staying in the land of the Kosalans in a certain forest grove.
translation_text.sn9.11:1.2: Now at that time that mendicant, during their day’s meditation, was thinking bad, unskillful thoughts, that is,
translation_text.sn9.11:1.4: The deity haunting that forest had sympathy for that mendicant, and wanted what’s best for them. So they approached that mendicant wanting to stir them up, and addressed them in verse:
translation_text.sn9.11:2.4: make sure your thoughts are rational.
translation_text.sn9.11:3.1: Thinking about the Teacher, the teaching,
translation_text.sn9.11:3.2: the Saṅgha, and your own ethics,
translation_text.sn9.11:3.4: and rapture and bliss as well, no doubt.
translation_text.sn9.11:3.5: And when you’re full of joy,
translation_text.sn9.11:3.6: you’ll make an end to suffering.”
translation_text.sn9.11:4.1: Impelled by that deity, that mendicant was struck with a sense of urgency.
